## Extraction Complete: EIP-7594 (PeerDAS - Peer Data Availability Sampling)

**Source**: `https://github.com/ethereum/EIPs/blob/master/EIPS/eip-7594.md`

**Output directory**: `/home/gohan/ga-runners/actions-runner-security-agent-3/_work/security-agent/security-agent/outputs/graphs/W0B1_1770272943/EIP-7594/`

### 7 Program Graphs Extracted

| ID | Name | Nodes | Edges | Invariants | File |
|:---|:-----|:-----:|:-----:|:----------:|:-----|
| SG-001 | `validate_blob_transaction_wrapper` | 11 | 17 | 4 | `SG-001_validate_blob_transaction_wrapper.mmd` |
| SG-002 | `data_availability_sampling` | 12 | 15 | 3 | `SG-002_data_availability_sampling.mmd` |
| SG-003 | `column_reconstruction` | 14 | 18 | 3 | `SG-003_column_reconstruction.mmd` |
| SG-004 | `node_custody_assignment` | 7 | 8 | 3 | `SG-004_node_custody_assignment.mmd` |
| SG-005 | `cell_proof_computation` | 6 | 7 | 3 | `SG-005_cell_proof_computation.mmd` |
| SG-006 | `blob_transaction_submission_validation` | 6 | 9 | 3 | `SG-006_blob_transaction_submission_validation.mmd` |
| SG-007 | `gossip_column_distribution` | 10 | 12 | 3 | `SG-007_gossip_column_distribution.mmd` |

### Graph Descriptions

**SG-001 - Blob Transaction Wrapper Validation**: The core validation logic when a node receives a `PooledTransactions` response. Validates count equality between versioned hashes, blobs, and commitments; enforces the 6-blob limit; verifies proof count matches `CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB * len(blobs)`; iterates over commitments to check `kzg_to_versioned_hash` matches; computes extension cells and batch-verifies all cell KZG proofs.

**SG-002 - Data Availability Sampling**: The per-slot DAS procedure. A node determines its custody groups, subscribes to column subnets via gossip, collects columns, and checks whether it has received enough samples (`SAMPLES_PER_SLOT`). If not, it requests missing columns from peers. Once sufficient columns are gathered, it verifies all sampled cells' KZG proofs.

**SG-003 - Column Reconstruction**: The data matrix reconstruction flow triggered when a node needs full data. Requires at least 50% of all columns (`NUMBER_OF_COLUMNS / 2`). If insufficient columns are available, it requests more from peers. Once the threshold is met, it iterates row-by-row, recovering and verifying each row against its KZG commitment.

**SG-004 - Node Custody Assignment**: Deterministic assignment of custody groups to nodes. Validators receive an additional custody bonus beyond the base `CUSTODY_REQUIREMENT`. Groups are derived deterministically from the node ID and mapped to column subnets.

**SG-005 - Cell Proof Computation**: The proof generation loop performed by blob transaction senders. Extends a blob via `compute_cells`, then iterates over all `CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB` cell indices to compute a KZG proof for each cell.

**SG-006 - Blob Transaction Submission Validation**: The entry-point validation when a blob transaction is submitted, received from the network, or processed during block production. Enforces the 6-blob limit, checks `wrapper_version == 1`, and delegates to the full wrapper validation (SG-001).

**SG-007 - Gossip Column Distribution**: The column publishing flow when a new block with blobs arrives. Extends all blobs, builds the column matrix, then iterates over all columns, publishing only those within the node's custody groups via gossip.

### 22 Invariants Identified

Key invariants include:
- **INV-001**: `len(blob_versioned_hashes) == len(blobs) == len(commitments)` (structural consistency)
- **INV-004/017**: Maximum 6 blobs per transaction
- **INV-008**: Reconstruction requires >= 50% of columns
- **INV-011**: Custody assignment is deterministic given node_id
- **INV-014**: Exactly `CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB` proofs per blob

The complete structured output is in `/home/gohan/ga-runners/actions-runner-security-agent-3/_work/security-agent/security-agent/outputs/graphs/W0B1_1770272943/EIP-7594/result.json`.

## Summary

**Specification**: EIP-7823 -- Set upper bounds for MODEXP

**Source**: `https://github.com/ethereum/EIPs/blob/master/EIPS/eip-7823.md`

EIP-7823 introduces an upper bound of 1024 bytes (8192 bits) on each of the three length inputs (`length_of_BASE`, `length_of_EXPONENT`, `length_of_MODULUS`) to the MODEXP precompile at address `0x05`. If any input length exceeds this bound, execution stops, returns an error, and consumes all remaining gas.

### Extracted Program Graphs (3 total)

| ID | Name | File | Description |
|:---|:-----|:-----|:------------|
| SG-001 | `modexp_input_decode` | `EIP-7823/SG-001_modexp_input_decode.mmd` | Sequential parsing of the 6 calldata fields (3 lengths + 3 values) |
| SG-002 | `modexp_bounds_check` | `EIP-7823/SG-002_modexp_bounds_check.mmd` | Three-stage validation that each length field is <= 1024 bytes, with error path consuming all gas |
| SG-003 | `modexp_precompile_execution` | `EIP-7823/SG-003_modexp_precompile_execution.mmd` | Full execution flow: decode, bounds check, gas calculation, modular exponentiation, and output |

### Key Invariants

- **INV-003**: Upper bound is 1024 bytes (8192 bits) for each of the three length inputs
- **INV-004**: If any length exceeds the bound, ALL gas is consumed before reverting
- **INV-006**: Bounds checking occurs BEFORE gas cost computation
- **INV-007**: Bounds violation consumes all gas (distinct from normal out-of-gas)

## Summary

**Specification**: EIP-7823 -- Set upper bounds for MODEXP

**Source**: `https://github.com/ethereum/EIPs/blob/master/EIPS/eip-7823.md`

EIP-7823 introduces an upper bound of 1024 bytes (8192 bits) on each of the three length inputs (`length_of_BASE`, `length_of_EXPONENT`, `length_of_MODULUS`) to the MODEXP precompile at address `0x05`. If any input length exceeds this bound, execution stops, returns an error, and consumes all remaining gas.

| SG-002 | `modexp_bounds_check` | `EIP-7823/SG-002_modexp_bounds_check.mmd` | Three-stage validation that each length field is <= 1024 bytes, with error path consuming all gas |
| SG-003 | `modexp_precompile_execution` | `EIP-7823/SG-003_modexp_precompile_execution.mmd` | Full execution flow: decode, bounds check, gas calculation, modular exponentiation, and output |

### Key Invariants

- **INV-003**: Upper bound is 1024 bytes (8192 bits) for each of the three length inputs
- **INV-004**: If any length exceeds the bound, ALL gas is consumed before reverting
- **INV-006**: Bounds checking occurs BEFORE gas cost computation
- **INV-007**: Bounds violation consumes all gas (distinct from normal out-of-gas)
